name: Mu Shu Pork #1
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Thinly sliced/shredded Chinese cabbage (bok choy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut pork into matchstick pieces.
2. Cut onions into 2-inch lengths, then cut the strips into shreds.
3. Heat a wok or large deep skillet over high heat. Add 1 tablespoon of oil and swirl to coat the bottom and sides.
4. Add onions, cabbage, bean sprouts, and bamboo shoots. Stir-fry until just wilted; remove to a large bowl.
5. Reheat the pan; add 2 more tablespoons of oil.
6. Add eggs, swirling the pan to spread out into thin layers. Cook until firm, breaking eggs up into small pieces. Remove to the bowl with vegetables.
7. Reheat the pan; add sesame seeds and remaining oil.
8. Add pork; stir-fry until browned and thoroughly cooked. Add soy sauce, salt, and sugar. Return vegetable-egg mixture to the pan. Stir-fry until heated.
9. Serve with mandarin pancakes. Have each person add about a tablespoon of filling to their own pancakes, adding a dab of hot mustard, oyster or plum sauce, or dipping sauce (from wonton recipe). Fold pancake in half like a soft taco, or roll it up, folding over one end to hold in the juices. Eat with fingers.
